items.title: These iPad apps will make you wish you had more free time

items.title: Apple made strides with iOS 26 security, but leaked hacking tools still leave millions exposed to spyware attacks
items.content: Leaked hacking tools threaten the security of millions of older iPhones. Cybersecurity experts weigh in.

items.title: Microsoft rolls back some of its Copilot AI bloat on Windows
items.content: The company is reducing Copilot entry points on Windows, starting with Photos, Widgets, Notepad, and other apps.

items.title: Microsoft hires the team of Sequoia-backed AI collaboration platform, Cove
items.content: AI collaboration startup Cove is shutting down after its team joined Microsoft, with service ending April 1 and customer data set for deletion.

items.title: The billion-dollar infrastructure deals powering the AI boom

items.title: The White House wants AI companies to cover rate hikes. Most have already said they would.
items.content: Many hyperscalers have already made public commitments to cover electricity cost increases.

items.title: He Was Supposed to Break Up Kraft and Heinz. He Changed His Mind.
items.content: Steve Cahillane, a food industry veteran, is trying to revive the company behind Kool-Aid, Oscar Mayer and many other brands.

items.title: The Fragile Hope for Salmon Recovery in Maine

items.title: Chromebook Remorse: Tech Backlash at Schools Extends Beyond Phones
items.content: No more YouTube or video games on school laptops. Textbooks and pencils are back. Some seventh graders say they prefer learning offline.
